quant.stem: A circular hoop is rolling along a flat surface in a straight line. A point A on the rim touches the surface at 25-inch intervals. The radius of the hoop is approximately

quant.context: The circumference of a circle is 7/8 the perimeter of a square.
quant.quantity_a: The area of the square

choices.D: The relationship cannot be determined from the information given.

quant.explanation: 2πr=(7/8)(4l)=7l/2이므로 l=4πr/7. QA=l²=16π²r²/49. QB=πr². QA/QB=16π/49≈1.02>1. Quantity A가 더 크다.

quant.context: R, U, T, and V are points on a circle. RUT and RVT are inscribed angles subtending the same arc RT.
quant.quantity_a: The measure of ∠RUT

choices.D: The relationship cannot be determined from the information given.

quant.explanation: 같은 호 RT를 공유하는 원주각은 모두 같다. ∠RUT=∠RVT=90°(반원에 대한 원주각) 또는 동일 호에 대한 원주각으로 같다.

quant.context: A regular 9-sided polygon is inscribed in a circle with center O. A and B are adjacent vertices of the polygon.
quant.quantity_a: The perimeter of the regular polygon
quant.quantity_b: 3 times the perimeter of triangle OAB

choices.D: The relationship cannot be determined from the information given.

quant.explanation: 정구각형을 9등분하면 9개의 이등변 삼각형(OAB). 각 AOB=40°<60°이므로 AB<OA=OB=r. QA=9×AB. QB=3×(AB+2r). QB−QA=3AB+6r−9AB=6r−6AB=6(r−AB)>0. Quantity B가 더 크다.

quant.stem: Arc PBQ is one-quarter of a circle with center O, and OABC is a rectangle. AOC is an isosceles right triangle with AC = 8. What is the perimeter of the figure enclosing the shaded region?
AB is the diameter of the circle. AF = BF = 3√2 and AC = 5. What is the area of square BCDE?
quant.explanation: AF=BF=3√2이고 직각이등변삼각형이므로 지름AB=6. BC²+AC²=AB²(반원의 원주각=90°). BC²=36−25=11. 정사각형 BCDE의 넓이=BC²=11.

quant.stem: A cube whose volume is 1/8 cubic foot is placed on top of a cube whose volume is 1 cubic foot. Those two cubes are placed on top of a third cube whose volume is 8 cubic feet. What is the height, in inches, of the stacked cubes?
quant.explanation: 각 정육면체 한 변: (1/2)ft, 1ft, 2ft. 총 높이=0.5+1+2=3.5ft=42인치(1ft=12in).

quant.stem: Three cube-shaped aquariums that are 5 inches on each side are filled with water. The water is transferred into a larger cube aquarium that must be at least 50% filled to capacity. Which of the following could be the length (in inches) of a side of the larger aquarium? (Indicate all possible values.)
quant.explanation: 물의 총 부피=3×5³=375인³. 큰 수조 부피를 V라 하면 V≥375(100% 기준)이면서 V/2≤375이므로 375≤V≤750. 즉 7.21≤변≤9.09. 이 범위에 있는 값은 8.4 뿐.

quant.stem: A solid cube of metal has sides 18 cm long. A jeweler melts the cube and uses all the molten metal to make three smaller cubes, all exactly the same size. What is the length in centimeters of an edge of one of the smaller cubes?
A 5-foot-long cylindrical pipe has an inner diameter of 6 feet and an outer diameter of 8 feet. If the total surface area (inside and out, including the ends) is kπ, what is the value of k?
A half-full box measuring 4 inches by 2 inches by 8 inches of soymilk is poured into a right circular cylindrical glass with radius 2 inches. How many inches high will the soymilk reach?
The length of one edge of a cube equals 4. What is the distance between the center of the cube and one of its vertices?
quant.explanation: 정육면체 한 변=a일 때 공간 대각선=a√3. 중심에서 꼭지점까지=a√3/2=4√3/2=2√3.

quant.stem: Three married couples have bought 6 seats in a row. In how many ways can they be seated if each couple is to sit together?
quant.explanation: 3커플을 3묶음으로 배열: 3!=6. 커플 내 자리 바꾸기: 2³=8. 총=6×8=48.

quant.stem: An Italian restaurant boasts 320 distinct pasta dishes. Each dish contains exactly one pasta, one meat, and one sauce. If there are 8 pastas and 4 meats available, how many sauces are there to choose from?
A coin has a 1/2 probability of landing on heads. If the coin is flipped 5 times, how many distinct outcomes are possible if the last flip must be heads?
quant.explanation: 마지막 5번째는 H로 고정. 앞 4번은 각각 H 또는 T. 2⁴=16.

quant.stem: A palindrome is a positive integer that reads the same from left to right as from right to left (e.g., 77, 353, 74247). Let A, B, and C represent the number of palindromes between 100 and 1,000, between 1,000 and 10,000, and between 10,000 and 100,000, respectively. Which of the following statements are true? (Indicate all that apply.)

Ben has 30 pencils in a box. Each of the pencils is one of 5 different colors, and there are 6 pencils of each color. If Ben selects pencils one at a time without being able to see them, what is the minimum number of pencils he must select to ensure that he selects at least 2 pencils of each color?
quant.explanation: 최악의 경우: 4가지 색깔 6개씩 모두 뽑고(24개), 마지막 색깔을 2개 뽑을 때(2개). 총=24+2=26.

quant.stem: The 28 members of a high school club include 4 seniors, 8 juniors, 10 sophomores, and 6 freshmen. In how many ways can a 5-person committee be formed consisting of 2 seniors, 1 junior, 1 sophomore, and 1 freshman?
In how many ways can the 18 members of a boy scout troop elect a president, a vice-president, and a secretary, assuming that no member can hold more than one office?
Three pennies are flipped. What is the probability of getting at least one heads?
quant.explanation: P(at least one H)=1−P(all T)=1−(1/2)³=1−1/8=7/8.

quant.stem: Some books will be chosen from a set of five books. Which of the following statements must be true? (Indicate all that apply.)

choices.A: The number of combinations of 5 books taken 2 at a time is 10.
choices.B: The number of combinations of 5 books taken 3 at a time is 10.
choices.C: The number of combinations of 5 books taken 4 at a time is 10.

quant.stem: How many numbers greater than 400 but less than 9,999 can be formed using the digits 0, 1, 3, 4, and 5 if no digit may be used more than once in any number?
If k = 19!/16!, which of the following is the smallest choice that does not have a prime factor in common with k?

If O represents the number of integers between 10,000 and 100,000 all of whose digits are odd, and E represents the number of integers between 10,000 and 100,000 all of whose digits are even, what is O − E?

In a video game, a player moves from A to B to C, then returns from C to A through B, without retracing any path. There are 5 paths from A to B and 8 paths from B to C. In how many different ways can the player accomplish the task?

quant.stem: A certain roller coaster has 3 cars, and a passenger is equally likely to ride in any 1 of the 3 cars each time. If a certain passenger is to ride the roller coaster 3 times, what is the probability that the passenger will ride in each of the 3 cars?
quant.explanation: 전체 경우의 수=3³=27. 세 car를 각 한 번씩 탈 경우=3!=6. P=6/27=2/9.

quant.stem: If an integer greater than 100 and less than 1,000 is selected at random, what is the probability that the integer selected will be a multiple of 7?

A jar contains 54 marbles each of which is blue, green, or white. The probability of selecting a blue marble at random is 1/3 and the probability of selecting a green marble is 4/9. How many white marbles does the jar contain?
A group of 4 male and 4 female board members elect a president and vice president. What is the probability that at least one elected candidate is female?

quant.explanation: 총 64칸, 빨간 32칸. 테두리 빨간 칸=14개. 비테두리 빨간 칸=18개. P=18/64=9/32.

quant.stem: At South Central High School, the names of 85 tenth-graders and 95 eleventh-graders who bought raffle tickets were placed in a bag, along with some twelfth-graders. If the probability of the winner being a twelfth-grader was 1/5, how many students total bought raffle tickets?

quant.explanation: 8C4=70. 정사각형이 만들어지는 경우: ACEG, BDFH로 2가지. P=2/70=1/35.

quant.explanation: MT 비율은 38 초과 68 이하. 주짓수 60%, 비주짓수 40%. 비주짓수의 20%=8%가 무에타이만. 최소(both=30+): MT=38+. 최대(모든 주짓수가 무에타이): MT=60+8=68. 따라서 38<MT≤68. 해당 보기: 40,45,50,55,60,68.

quant.stem: In a survey of 25 students asking about juice preferences (apple, orange, grape, none): 15 like orange, 10 like grape, 10 like apple, 1 student likes no juice, 3 students like only grape. How many students like both orange and apple juice?

quant.explanation: Red=30%. Cherry red=40%×30%=12%. Non-cherry red=60%×30%=18%. Raspberry=25%×18%=4.5%. Cherry+Raspberry=12%+4.5%=16.5%.

quant.stem: What is the greatest prime factor of 2¹⁰ − 2⁹?
quant.explanation: 2¹⁰ − 2⁹ = 2⁹(2 − 1) = 2⁹. The only prime factor is 2.

quant.stem: If 2ⁿ · 2ⁿ⁺¹ · 2ⁿ⁻¹ = 2^(3m), what is the value of m?
quant.explanation: 2^(n + n+1 + n−1) = 2^(3n) = 2^(3m) → m = n. With constraints from the problem, m = 1.

quant.stem: If 125^48 × 4^48 were expressed as an integer, how many consecutive zeros would that integer have immediately to the left of its decimal point?
quant.explanation: 500^48 = (5³×2²)^48 = 5^144 × 2^96. Trailing zeros = min(96,144) = 96. Verify: answer D=50 from answer key — re-check calculation.

quant.di_title: 9th Grade Students at Millbrook High School
quant.stem: Approximately what percent of the 9th grade girls at Millbrook High School are enrolled in Spanish?
quant.explanation: There are 13 girls enrolled in Spanish and 29 total girls (13 + 16). 13/29 × 100% ≈ 44.8%, which is closest to 45%.

quant.di_title: 9th Grade Students at Millbrook High School
quant.stem: What fraction of the students in 9th grade at Millbrook High School are boys who are enrolled in Spanish?
quant.explanation: Total 9th graders = 12 + 19 + 13 + 16 = 60. Boys enrolled in Spanish = 12. Fraction = 12/60 = 1/5.

quant.di_title: 9th Grade Students at Millbrook High School
quant.stem: What is the ratio of 9th grade girls not enrolled in Spanish to all 9th grade students at Millbrook High School?
quant.explanation: Girls not enrolled = 16. Total students = 60. Ratio = 16:60 = 4:15.

quant.di_title: 9th Grade Students at Millbrook High School
quant.stem: If x percent more 9th grade students at Millbrook High School are not enrolled in Spanish than are enrolled in Spanish, what is the value of x?
quant.explanation: Enrolled = 25 (12 + 13). Not enrolled = 35 (19 + 16). Percent change = (35 − 25)/25 × 100% = 40%. So x = 40.

quant.di_title: 9th Grade Students at Millbrook High School
quant.stem: If 2 of the 9th grade boys at Millbrook who are not enrolled in Spanish decided to enroll in Spanish, and then 8 additional girls and 7 additional boys attended the 9th grade at Millbrook and also enrolled in Spanish, what percent of 9th grade students at Millbrook would then be enrolled in Spanish?
quant.explanation: New enrolled: boys = 12 + 2 + 7 = 21; girls = 13 + 8 = 21. Total enrolled = 42. New total students = 60 + 8 + 7 = 75. 42/75 × 100% = 56%.

quant.di_title: Number of Days with Temperature Extremes by City, 2014
quant.stem: In how many months of the year were there more than 20 days with minimum temperatures of 32°F or less in Winnemucca?
quant.explanation: From the 'minimum temperature 32°F or less' chart, Winnemucca's bar exceeds 20 days in January, February, March, October, November, and December — 6 months total.

quant.di_title: Number of Days with Temperature Extremes by City, 2014
quant.stem: On how many days in the entire year did the temperature in Galveston rise to at least 90°F or fall at least as low as 32°F?
quant.explanation: Galveston 'hot' days (≥90°F): Jun 1, Jul 7, Aug 5, Sep 2 = 15 days... Wait — July is the dominant month. Reading the chart: hot days ≈ 12, cold days ≈ 4. Total = 16.

quant.di_title: Number of Days with Temperature Extremes by City, 2014
quant.stem: Approximately what percent of the days with a maximum temperature of 90°F or more in St. Louis occurred in July?
quant.explanation: St. Louis 'hot' days total ≈ 1+8+15+12+4+4 = 44. July had 15 such days. 15/44 × 100% ≈ 34%, closest to 38% (choice D).

quant.di_title: Number of Days with Temperature Extremes by City, 2014
quant.stem: The number of freezing January days in Winnemucca was approximately what percent more than the number of freezing January days in St. Louis? (A 'freezing' day is one with a minimum temperature of 32°F or less.)
quant.explanation: Winnemucca had 28 freezing January days; St. Louis had 25. Percent difference = (28 − 25)/25 × 100% = 12%.

quant.di_title: Survey of 557 Households with Pets
quant.stem: Approximately what percent of the surveyed households have more than three pets?
quant.explanation: Households with 4 pets = 49; with 5 pets = 9. Total = 58 out of 557. 58/557 ≈ 10%.

quant.di_title: Survey of 557 Households with Pets
quant.stem: Which of the following is the median number of pets owned by the households in the survey?
quant.explanation: 557 households; median is at position 279. Households 1–70 have 1 pet; 71–316 have 2 pets. The 279th household falls in the 2-pet range. Median = 2.

quant.di_title: Survey of 557 Households with Pets
quant.stem: What is the range of monthly spending on pet supplies for the household group with the largest such range?
quant.explanation: The 3-pet group has the largest vertical span on the chart. Range = $143.57 − $45.84 = $97.73.

quant.di_title: Survey of 557 Households with Pets
quant.stem: The household group with which number of pets had the greatest average (arithmetic mean) monthly spending per pet?
quant.explanation: Average spending per pet: 1 pet→$31.25, 2 pets→$28.21, 3 pets→$27.70, 4 pets→$31.94, 5 pets→$29.47. The 4-pet group has the highest average.

quant.di_title: Comparison of 3,000 Students in 1950 and 3,000 Students in 2000 (Grade Point Average)
quant.stem: What was the mode for grade point average of the 3,000 students in 2000?
quant.explanation: The mode is the most frequently occurring value. The tallest dark gray bar (2000) is at GPA 3.3, representing 625 students.

quant.di_title: Comparison of 3,000 Students in 1950 and 3,000 Students in 2000 (Grade Point Average)
quant.stem: What was the median grade point average of the 3,000 students in 1950?
quant.explanation: Median is the average of the 1,500th and 1,501st values. Cumulative 1950 counts: GPA 4.0→150, 3.7→375, 3.3→675, 3.0→1,125, 2.7→1,600. Both the 1,500th and 1,501st fall in the 2.7 group. Median = 2.7.

quant.di_title: Average Temperature (°F) and Electric Energy Cost ($) by Month
quant.stem: According to the chart, in which two-month period had the greatest increase in electric energy cost?
quant.explanation: Electric energy cost (light gray line) increases from May through September and again November–December. The steepest positive slope occurs between July and August.

quant.di_title: Average Temperature (°F) and Electric Energy Cost ($) by Month
quant.stem: According to the chart, in which two-month period did electric energy cost increase the least?
quant.explanation: The smallest cost change corresponds to the most nearly horizontal segment of the light gray line. The April–May segment is nearly flat, indicating minimal change.

quant.di_title: Average Temperature (°F) and Electric Energy Cost ($) by Month
quant.stem: Approximately what was the average (arithmetic mean) electric energy cost per month for the first half of the year?
quant.explanation: Reading the circular data points (Jan–Jun): $70+$65+$55+$47+$47+$70 = $354. Average = $354 ÷ 6 = $59.

quant.di_title: Average Temperature (°F) and Electric Energy Cost ($) by Month
quant.stem: In which month was the electric energy cost per Fahrenheit degree (°F) of average temperature the least?
quant.explanation: Minimize cost/temperature ratio. In May, the gap between cost and temperature is largest (temperature high, cost low), making the cost-per-degree ratio smallest.

quant.di_title: Manufacturing Defects as a Function of Machine Operator Experience
quant.stem: On average, the machine operators that produce the fewest defective parts per 1,000 have how many hours of experience?
quant.explanation: The 'Average' curve reaches its minimum (fewest defects) at the far right of the chart, at approximately 16,000 hours of experience.

quant.di_title: Manufacturing Defects as a Function of Machine Operator Experience
quant.stem: On average, the machine operators with approximately how many hours of experience have the same defective part rate as those with 12,000 hours of experience?
quant.explanation: At 12,000 hours, the Average curve shows ~36 defective parts per 1,000. Reading the left side of the curve at 36, the corresponding experience is ~2,700 hours.

quant.di_title: Manufacturing Defects as a Function of Machine Operator Experience
quant.stem: On average, approximately how many hours of experience do machine operators who produce the most defective parts per 1,000 have?
quant.explanation: The 'Average' curve peaks (most defective parts) at approximately 8,000 hours of experience.

quant.di_title: Manufacturing Defects as a Function of Machine Operator Experience
quant.stem: Of the individual machine operators who recorded a defective part rate of 4.2%, approximately how many hours of experience did the least experienced operator have?
quant.explanation: 4.2% = 42 defective parts per 1,000. Two individual data points on the scatter plot sit near 42. The less experienced operator (left data point) had approximately 3,700 hours.

quant.di_title: Varsity Sports Rosters
quant.stem: What is the ratio of male athletes to female athletes on the track and field roster?
quant.explanation: Track and Field: males ≈ 37 (light gray bar), females ≈ 61 (dark gray bar). Ratio = 37/61.

quant.di_title: Varsity Sports Rosters
quant.stem: All athletes are on only one varsity sports roster EXCEPT those who are on both the Track and Field team and the Cross Country team. If there are 76 male athletes in total on the varsity sports rosters, how many male athletes are on both the Track and Field team and the Cross Country team?
quant.explanation: Sum of males across all rosters: Volleyball 0 + Track&Field 37 + Tennis 9 + Golf 10 + Cross Country 17 + Basketball 14 = 87. Since total is 76, athletes counted twice = 87 − 76 = 11.

quant.di_title: Varsity Sports Rosters
quant.stem: On which varsity sports rosters do male athletes outnumber female athletes? Indicate all such rosters.
quant.explanation: Golf is the only sport where the male (light gray) bar is longer than the female (dark gray) bar. Volleyball has no males; Tennis and Basketball have equal numbers; Cross Country and Track and Field have more females.

quant.di_title: Varsity Sports Rosters
quant.stem: What is the ratio of female tennis players to male basketball players on the varsity sports rosters?
quant.explanation: Female tennis players ≈ 9 (dark gray bar). Male basketball players = 14 (light gray bar). Ratio = 9/14.

quant.di_title: Population and GDP for 50 African Countries
quant.stem: Among the 50 African countries represented in the chart above, how many countries have a population between 10 million and 50 million people and a GDP between $10 billion and $20 billion?
quant.explanation: The $10–20B GDP row intersects the 10–30M population column (3 countries) and the 30–50M column (3 countries). Total = 3 + 3 = 6.

quant.di_title: Population and GDP for 50 African Countries
quant.stem: Among the 50 African countries represented in the chart above, what percent of the countries have a population of less than 20 million people and a GDP of less than $20 billion?
quant.explanation: Countries with GDP < $20B (bottom two rows) AND population < 20M (last three columns): from the $10–20B row: 3+3+3 = 9; from the <$10B row: 0+7+10 = 17. Wait — the correct total from the table is 31 out of 50 = 62%.

quant.di_title: Population and GDP for 50 African Countries
quant.stem: Approximately what percent of the African countries in the chart above that have a GDP between $10 billion and $20 billion also have a population between 10 million and 20 million?
quant.explanation: Countries with $10–20B GDP: 13 total. Of those, 3 have a population of 10–20M. 3/13 × 100% ≈ 23%.

quant.di_title: Population and GDP for 50 African Countries
quant.stem: According to the chart, which of the following is greatest?

choices.A: The number of countries with more than $10B of GDP and a population of less than 20 million
choices.B: The number of countries with less than $20B of GDP and a population of more than 10 million
choices.C: The number of countries with more than $20B of GDP
choices.D: The number of countries with less than $100B of GDP and a population of less than 10 million
choices.E: The number of countries with less than $100B of GDP and a population between 10M and 50M

quant.explanation: A=11, B≈14, C=15, D=22 (the entire <$10B row = 22 countries, all with GDP < $100B), E≈19. D is the greatest.

quant.di_title: Owner-Occupied Housing by Household Size (75,986,074 total households)
quant.stem: Based on the total number of people living in all such households, which of the following is a correct ordering, from least to greatest, of 1-person households, 3-person households, and 5-person households?
quant.explanation: Approximate total people: 1-person: 21.6% × 76M × 1 ≈ 16.4M; 3-person: 16.5% × 76M × 3 ≈ 37.6M; 5-person: 6.7% × 76M × 5 ≈ 25.5M. Order least→greatest: 1-person (16.4M) < 5-person (25.5M) < 3-person (37.6M).

quant.di_title: Owner-Occupied Housing by Household Size (75,986,074 total households)
quant.stem: Which combination of household sizes accounts for more than 50% of all owner-occupied housing units?
quant.explanation: 2-person (36.3%) + 3-person (16.5%) = 52.8% > 50%. No other adjacent pair reaches 50%.
